commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] A focused automated repair can address the shared-context propagation bug, but contributor real behavior proof remains a separate merge gate.; Needs attention: The patch changes a security-boundary policy signal and currently can under-report same-run external content to policy hooks.; Review finding: [P1] Use one run-shared context for provenance — src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts:1376 (sha=8121fdb2cce09ebf383a267c8f0ea46c00aaca98)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an optional `externalContent` provenance signal to plugin `before_tool_call` event/context types, embedded-runner hook context assembly, trusted policy events, docs, and regression tests.

PR surface: Source +279, Tests +149, Docs +8. Total +436 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: production core tools are wrapped with per-tool contexts before the PR's shared catalog context exists, so mutating one wrapper after a `web_fetch` result cannot update a later `exec` or `apply_patch` wrapper. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A focused automated repair can address the shared-context propagation bug, but contributor real behavior proof remains a separate merge gate.

target.title: fix(tools): propagate external content provenance to policy hooks
target.body:
Summary:
- Adds structured external-content provenance to tool-call policy hook types.
- Propagates provenance through HookContext, before_tool_call plugin events, trusted tool policy events, and tool hook context.
- Detects EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT markers from the active embedded run prompt/session context.
- Refreshes provenance after same-run tool results, so fetch/browser/file external content is visible to later policy hooks in the same run.
- Documents the public hook field and source semantics.
- Refreshes the plugin SDK type barrel so extension package-boundary cache keys invalidate when hook contract shapes change.

Why:
- External content is already wrapped as untrusted prompt text.
- Before this change, the post-model tool-call policy boundary did not receive structured provenance showing whether external content was present.
- Policy plugins could see the tool name, params, tool kind/input kind, run/toolCallId, derived paths, and session context, but not whether the tool call followed browser, web_fetch, email, webhook, file-fetch, or other untrusted external context.

Behavior proof:
- Added a same-run fetch-then-exec regression.
- The first tool call simulates a web_fetch result containing EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT with `Source: Web Fetch`.
- The later exec tool call uses the same wrapped HookContext.
- The before_tool_call hook receives `externalContent: { present: true, sources: ["web_fetch"] }` on both the event and context for the later exec call.
- The first web_fetch call has no externalContent field before the fetch result is returned, proving the provenance is introduced by the external tool result and not pre-seeded manually.

Validation:
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
- pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile
- git diff --check
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
